human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for dealer-support boundary discipline, source quality, VIN and parts traceability, quality-hold and stop-sale behavior, supplier and substitution boundaries, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to automotive dealer warranty and product-service support, including VIN, repair-order, DTC, labor-op, campaign, causal-part, parts, traceability, and escalation handling.

Authority sources cover FTC warranty-law guidance, current eCFR warranty and recall rules, current NHTSA recall workflow guidance, AIAG warranty and traceability references, IATF supplier-quality materials, GS1 traceability, and current OEM public workflow evidence.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, policy exceptions, repeat failures, traceability conflict, quality hold and stop-sale boundaries, supplier-substitution pressure, adjacent-lane routing, unsupported closure, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/automotive-and-mobility/dealer-support-specialist/ so dealer support remains separate from quality, planning, inventory, procurement, supplier quality, and logistics lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around policy exceptions, supplier approval, quality holds, stop-sale posture, and unsupported claim closure.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated dealer support specialist that supports evidence-backed warranty and service handling, traceability-aware parts and hold analysis, and authority-safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
